calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-30-2008 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-02-2008 Continued To Jun-30-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for Rebecca Mead.

Mr. Senick stated that he thought the matter was resolved, that Victoria had enclosed the check and a request for payment of fees. In addition, he stated that they agreed to pay Victoria $5,479.47 for reimbursement of gas and for care of the Decedent's cat and that the only issue remaining was the receipt on distribution from Cheryl Poncett. Mr. Senick is to file a declaration of due diligence in regard to the receipt and a supplement. The matter was continued to June 30, 2008 for status on the Final Distribution.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lazatin.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-12-2008 Continued To Jun-02-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for Rebecca Mead, Petitioner; Victoria Mead present in pro per (Beneficiary).

Ms. Victoria Mead stated that the Estate had not been administered and that she was concerned that Rebecca Mead failed to take care of the properties, that no inventory was filed and that she had not received any reimbursements for her expenses. The Court noted that there was no objection filed or any formal pleading on those issues. Mr. Senick stated that the Inventory was filed, that any unwanted property went to Good Will and that Victoria was satisfied with the distribution of personal property. In addition, he stated that a $4,800.00 check was cashed by Victoria for hours of work done to help with the clean up, but that no bills were submitted by Victoria or her attorney from New York regarding the funeral's expenses. He also stated that Victoria had not signed the receipt for the $75,000.00 distribution. The Court directed all parties to furhter discuss the matter outside the courtroom. On the second call of the calendar, Victoria agreed to forward a copy of the receipts to Mr. Senick and to sign the receipt for the $75,000.00 distribution. Interest requested by Victoria and attorney fees will be further discussed along with the value of the car and repairs. The matter was continued to June 2, 2008 for filing the supplement of the accounting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.
